channel.title: The Good Globe
channel.description: The Good Globe publishes only genuinely good news from around the world — real stories of kindness, scientific breakthroughs, nature recovering and communities winning, selected every day. No doom, no fear, no outrage.

item.title: The Iberian lynx is back from the brink of extinction
item.description: Once down to fewer than a hundred animals, the world's rarest wild cat has multiplied more than twentyfold and is no longer endangered.

item.title: Millions of children are now protected by malaria vaccines
item.description: Two safe, effective vaccines are rolling out across Africa, reaching millions of children in the fight against one of humanity's oldest killers.

item.title: Surgeons performed the world's first whole-eye transplant
item.description: A team in New York transplanted an entire human eye for the first time — and it stayed healthy, opening a path toward restoring sight.

item.title: The world is installing clean energy faster than ever
item.description: Solar panels and wind turbines are being added at record pace every year — renewables now dominate all new power built worldwide.

item.title: Deforestation in the Amazon has fallen sharply
item.description: Satellite monitoring shows forest loss in the Brazilian Amazon dropping steeply since 2023 — the rainforest is getting room to breathe.

item.title: The ozone layer is healing — on track for full recovery
item.description: UN scientists confirm the ozone layer is steadily recovering thanks to a worldwide ban on harmful chemicals — proof cooperation works.

item.title: Notre-Dame stands restored, welcoming millions once more
item.description: Five years after the fire, Paris's cathedral opened its doors again — rebuilt by thousands of craftspeople and visited by millions.
